title: bitwarden: N+2 API calls per get; project_id and secret_id not cached
description: Every get() call (after the one-time auth) makes 3 API calls: projects().list() (O(projects)), secrets().list_by_project() (O(secrets_in_project)), then secrets().get(). The project_id and secret_id are resolved by name-search on every call rather than being cached. Scenario: a user calls get() in a loop or via CachingStore::refresh() — each refresh is 3 Bitwarden API calls. With moderate call frequency, this will trigger Bitwarden rate limits and generate confusing Backend errors. The maintenance consequence is a stream of issue reports about rate limiting that trace back to architecture, not user error. Fix: cache the (project_id, secret_id) pair in a OnceCell alongside the auth client, or document that CachingStore with a non-zero TTL is required.
